title: tep_hierarchy_assessment.txt
summary: Plain-text export of the 'tep_hierarchy_assessment' Google Doc (id 1so1AruT0...), content verified identical. 'Consolidated Assessment: TEP Budget Equation, Spectral Moment Hierarchy, and the Trans-Critical Regime' (Working Document, Feb 2026). Establishes the exact qubit G-factor formula for spectral dispersion F = p3 - gamma^2 = (x/sqrt(2))^2 / G^2(sqrt(2)x) with a metric horizon at x=1/sqrt(2) (gamma_GMC=1) where the purity speed limit |dgamma/dt| <= 4||V|| sqrt(F) shuts off; shows a trans-critical regime exists for d>=3 (x_max=sqrt(2/3)) regularized by independent p3; interprets horizons as participation-ratio relevance thresholds (PR=k at x=sqrt(1-1/k), bidirectional crossover not phase transition); critically assesses TEP (budget equation 'barely' non-tautological for qubits, substantive as approximation for d>=3; Hartmann short-time connection real but needs sharpening; GMC dictionary a promising analogy pending tau(q)/Wishart proof; freezing transition at gamma_GMC=2 inaccessible to finite d). Concludes budget = leading-order log-normal description, hierarchy = correction series, and recommends the hierarchy paper stand alone with TEP as discussion only.

path: TEP 2026-07/v0.5 — Terminal Equivalence Principle (TEP) & Passive Holography — Working Notes .txt
summary: The foundational v0.5 working notes defining the Terminal Equivalence Principle: eliminating a linear, reciprocal, passive interior via Schur/Kron reduction yields a symmetric positive-real boundary law f_b = Lambda_eff(s) u_b, so interior curl/orthogonal dynamics re-express as boundary divergence/collinear law with memory. Companion principles are laid out with proofs and appendices A-P: Passive Holography (the DtN operator encodes all external observables; cycle-only surgeries like star-mesh/Kron are holographically equivalent), Constitutive Momentum Equivalence (in K/I media with c^2=K/I_eff, Abraham and Minkowski momenta coincide, g = I_eff(ExB)), Budget-Curvature Correspondence (metric dSigma^2 = u c^2 dt^2 - u^{-1} dl^2 with u = PE/(KE+PE)), the orbit limit (G=0, B!=0, unitary scattering), a log kit (rapidity eta = (1/2)ln(PE/KE)), universal near-critical fold scaling of Goos-Hanchen shift and Wigner delay as (1-s^2)^{-1/2}, conserved boundary phase current with integer topological charges, RG flow on Lambda_eff, boundary connectors C_0/DeltaK/C_Lambda, budget Snell-Fermat law, and Abel-inversion boundary tomography for u(x). It also gives a simulation spec for a graded K/I slab, an experimental/measurement ID checklist (KK-relation consistency, reactive slope tests for sigma and D), and touchstones (BEC/polaritons, graded metamaterials, thermal plates). Documented limitations: Part X (algebraic-geometry 'TEP for chain complexes') is explicitly flagged highly speculative; holography 'escapes' via nonreciprocity and topological invariants are acknowledged; capillarity formulas are small-slope with sign-convention caveats; loss/anisotropy cut off the universal divergences and smear integer charges to phase deficits; the version note states it integrates former Appendices N/O and PR Schur, DtN symbol, and port-Hamiltonian appendices from v0.3.
references: v0.3 of the same working notes (explicitly updated/integrated); IFFT/CME dictionary (Appendix L)

title: 2026-07-15 faces_seam_groupoid_record_Delta2.md
summary: Delta-2 consolidating turns 13-20 of the Collatz x TEP thread. Face swaps: Collatz word as S-matrix (Schur swap; cycle = integer-quantized resonance r' = n(1 - tt')); EP-locking (the monoid lives on the exceptional locus, Baker as linewidth protection); Wick swap (elliptic face hosts no arithmetic cycles by Gelfond); level-transfer lemma tying involution expulsions to the 3x+d level tower. Proves the seam's symbolic locus is the Sturmian subshift of slope log_3(2) with exact ledger law and closed-form seam holonomy density 1/(2 ln 3), plus the integer-freeness theorem-let. Introduces the transmission groupoid with the central/twisted peel bifurcation, identifies the Petz section on the word fiber as the Sturmian representative, and reads the Collatz seam as the ghost of the impossible multiplicative peel (log_2 3 irrational). Contains a retraction ledger (nu2 slot, Levin conservation, Lévy-area valence) and the anti-Sisyphean process rule.
references: 2026-07-15 collatz_tep_division_of_labor_record_ActiveV1 (parent); 2026-07-15 A4 delta + addendum; 2026-06-25 record (silver nesting priority); 2026-05-30 record

title: 2026-07-15 collatz_tep_division_of_labor_record_ActiveV1.md
summary: Nine-turn record asking whether TEP is a structural guide or a dictionary for Collatz. Establishes the semidirect decomposition <D,U> = Lambda x| <2,3> (abelian ledger vs path-ordered N-cocycle; cycle condition = holonomy pays the abelian gap); the anholonomy trichotomy placing Collatz at the parabolic seam it never occupies (Kesten-critical, elliptic region empty); the (A) boundedness / (B) cycle-inventory factorization with (A) = pointwise seam recurrence; the sign channel via the Fricke template; and the transmission-functor reading (shortcut map = unit-rate erasure channel on Z2, Kraft-tight code, thinning 0.0793 bits/step). Verdict-to-date: dictionary, not yet guide, with three falsifiable upgrade conditions. Logs failed predictions (Levin conservation, rough-path valence) per two-ledger discipline.
references: 2026-05-30 Thomas record; 2026-06-28 consolidation record; 2026-07-03 delta (Fricke/iota laws); 2026-06-25 record; section 4 superseded by 2026-07-15 A4 Delta1

title: 2026-07-15 A4_selection_rule_resolution_Delta1.md
summary: Delta resolving anchor A4 of the Collatz division-of-labor record: two no-go lemmas (the end-separating character dies on the 2,3-divisible anholonomy lattice; Hedlund's theorem makes the parity-flip F-tilde the unique continuous commuting involution, and it breaks integrality), so no pointwise Fricke analogue exists. Constructs F-tilde, runs the complete flip census to n=13 (five known integral orbits; -5 orbit maps to {4/5, 2/5, 1/5}; -17 orbit to denominator 1967), and installs the replacement law: end = sign(2^n - 3^K), ledger-valued not character-valued. A4 splits into A4a (promoted firm) and A4b (retired with proof); supersedes section 4 of the parent record.
references: 2026-07-15 collatz_tep_division_of_labor_record_ActiveV1 (parent; supersedes its section 4); 2026-07-05 buhler_control_PASS; 2026-07-03 delta (Fricke selection rule)

title: 2026-07-15 A4_reversal_chirality_Delta1_Addendum1.md
summary: Addendum to the A4 delta prompted by Will's audit: admits the no-go covered pointwise involutions only, then checks the direction-reversal anti-automorphism R (word reversal, M -> JM^T J on transfer matrices). Finds the -17 orbit is chiral with R-partner -20632/139 expelled from Z (denominators {5, 139, 1967} characteristic), while the other four integral orbits are achiral; the chirality bit is the precise information lost to direction-symmetrization. Builds the direction x content ledger (metabelian G: G^ab = ledger, [G,G] = anholonomy), separates solution-locus end (abelian, exact seam-sign law) from system/mirror end (non-abelian, not character-readable), and instantiates recoverability = solvability literally via the derived-length-2 two-read recovery.

summary: The TEP/Entrodynamics Central Reference: a tiered-rigor consolidation explicitly written to replace earlier promotional synthesis documents. Tier 1 theorems: rank-2 purity transport (|dgamma/dt| <= 4||H||sqrt(F), F=p3-gamma^2, rank-2 bivector W, constructive qubit saturation), Kerr-Newman budget identity (r-hat_+-1)^2+j^2+q-hat^2=1, sharp operator as half-rapidity map, coupling generator composition law k_X k_Y = k^2_sqrt(XY), Compton-Schwarzschild product lambda_C r_s = 2 l_P^2, the moment-ratio hierarchy x_k^2 = p_k^2/(p_{k-1}p_{k+1}) resolving the multiple-budget-equation inconsistency, and the three-term budget x^2+w+u=1 from passivity+reciprocity. Tier 2: Schwarzschild horizon at x^2=1/2, critical damping zeta^2=1/2, Chicone-Mashhoon c/sqrt(2) (a genuine pre-literature predictive hit). Tier 3: gamma_c(d)=1/(d-1), corrected HBAR bimetric comparison (gamma*=2-sqrt(2), distinct from gamma_c), commensurability index, mass budget x_m=r_s/(r_s+lambda_C). Tier 5 retires: original HBAR framing, blind extraction u-dot/(2x^2), DD G-factor scaling, the rational sieve, 'TEP derives gravity', and Standard Model mappings; documents the 'LLM-confirmation failure mode' and full corpus provenance (GSR->IFFT->TEP lineage, primary sources tep_session_record_derivation.txt, O_R__2-09-26.txt, TEP_Silver_Ratio_Findings.md).

title: 2026-05-15 III_comprehensive_investigation_record.txt
path: TEP 2026-07/Dated TEP Documents ~2026/2026-05-15 I thru III comprehensive_investigation_record/2026-05-15 III_comprehensive_investigation_record.txt
summary: Third continuation record ('the previous two comprehensive records documented the technical content') covering the framework's 'foundational reckoning': its promotion from structural reorganization to the explicit working-out of universal information-geometric content grounded in Chentsov's theorem (operational measurement -> probability space -> Fisher-Rao uniqueness -> framework apparatus, verified on the 2-state simplex where the budget identity X^2 + U = 1 is a coordinate expression of Fisher-Rao). Documents the arrival of the parallel-conversation Algebraic Architecture document centered on the involution f(y) = sqrt(1-y^2), the layered three-budget DHO architecture with bridge identity G^2 - 1 = product of lower layers, an imaginary-structure taxonomy, the Goos-Hanchen layered-G-product derivation, a seven-candidate counterexample hunt (all accommodated), and the ABJ chiral anomaly coefficient derivation c_n = 1/(n!(2pi)^n) (1/(8pi^2) in 4D) from the T^4 = id four-cycle plus BCH/Maurer-Cartan factors. Records both over- and under-claiming corrections and recommends leading publications with the Chentsov grounding. Does not mention the 05-14 series.
references: 2026-05-15 I_comprehensive_investigation_record; 2026-05-15 II_comprehensive_investigation_record; Algebraic Architecture document (from parallel conversation)

title: 2026-05-15 II_comprehensive_investigation_record.txt
path: TEP 2026-07/Dated TEP Documents ~2026/2026-05-15 I thru III comprehensive_investigation_record/2026-05-15 II_comprehensive_investigation_record.txt
summary: Explicit continuation ('picks up where the first comprehensive record left off') resolving the status of the cubic Haldane term g(1-g)n^3. Headline Tier-1 result: full Haldane fractional exclusion statistics emerge exactly as the NSR-additive convex composition 1/n_H = (1-g)/n_B + g/n_F of BE and FD channels at offset depths with unit NSR gap (verified to 1e-16), so no higher-order Schur reduction or matrix Riccati on the Siegel disk is needed - both attempted extensions are documented as failed hypotheses and retired. Also establishes a two-composition theorem (equal-depth composition gives the scalar-Riccati fractional family, distinguishing independent boson-fermion mixtures from anyonic single-species statistics), a Casimir-deficit reading g(1-g) = (1-c^2)/4, a rank-2 wedge/Haldane bilinear correspondence, and a bidisk half-depth factorization n_BE(s) = n_BE(s/2)*n_FD(s/2); one methodological lapse is documented and corrected. Continues Record I of the same 05-15 series; does not mention the 05-14 series.
references: 2026-05-15 I_comprehensive_investigation_record (explicit continuation); Central Reference (Methodological Notes / tier structure)

title: 2026-05-15 I_comprehensive_investigation_record.txt
path: TEP 2026-07/Dated TEP Documents ~2026/2026-05-15 I thru III comprehensive_investigation_record/2026-05-15 I_comprehensive_investigation_record.txt
summary: First of a three-part 'Comprehensive Investigation Record' series consolidating a single-conversation structural investigation of the TEP/Entrodynamics framework's thermal-statistical content, starting from an assessment of two 'tonally overeager' synthesis documents. Derives the Riccati flow dn/ds = -n - n^2 on the Poincare disk from passive scattering (SU(1,1), Iwasawa decomposition, capacity-additive NSR depth coordinate), classifies BE/MB/FD as (a,b,c) Riccati orbits with the Pauli bound as an upper fixed point, and establishes the headline Tier-1 result that the framework's continuous-c family is the dilute-limit asymptotic of Haldane fractional exclusion statistics with correction g(1-g)/(2e^s). Also numerically tests the Hodge decomposition of the passivity budget w + x^2 + u = 1 and corrects the Central Reference V2's sector labels (transmission is harmonic under reciprocity, not co-exact), stratifies four cascade families as sl(2,R) generators with distinct thermodynamic regimes, and retires the Friis-to-Fermi-Dirac conjecture. Does not mention the 2026-05-14 investigation-record series; a similarly-titled I-III series dated 05-14 exists elsewhere, so duplication should be checked.
references: Central Reference V2 (Hodge labels corrected, tier structure adopted); two synthesis documents assessed as tonally overeager

summary: A transcript with Kimi assessing the user's information-logic postulate. The user presents x^2+u = ESS/TSS + RSS/TSS = 1 (coherence as R^2), the consonance/dissonance measurement-difficulty idea, Planck units as metric fundaments, and the G(x)=dA/dx=sec A derivation of hyperbolicity. Kimi's verdict: the budget identity is a profound isomorphism (Cramer-Rao bound structure), not a derivation; the secant form of G(x) is chosen, not forced, unless a multiplicative compositionality theorem G(x1 o x2)=G(x1)G(x2) is proven; the nested vantage-point strategy is hierarchical Bayesian inference with dissonance as log-condition number.
